title: Kruskal's Algorithm
description: Given an undirected weighted graph, find the total weight of the Minimum Spanning Tree using Kruskal's algorithm with Union-Find.
key_concept: Sort all edges by weight. Greedily add cheapest edge that doesn't form a cycle. Union-Find detects cycles in O(α(n)).
when_best: Sparse graphs (E << V²), when edges are already sorted or need global minimum first

hints.L1: Sort edges by weight. Use Union-Find to detect cycles. Add edge if the two endpoints are in different components.
hints.L2: DSU with path compression + union by rank. For each edge (u,v,w) in sorted order: if find(u) != find(v): union(u,v); total += w; stop when n-1 edges added.
hints.L3: Sort edges; init DSU; total=0; edges_used=0; for w,u,v in edges: if find(u)!=find(v): union(u,v); total+=w; edges_used++; if edges_used==n-1: break; return total
